blocks.title: Let's understand how you think and act

questions.text: Your group project is a total mess — wrong direction, deadline tomorrow. What do you do?

options.label: Take charge and redirect the whole team
Quietly fix my part and hope others follow
Talk to each person individually to align everyone
Raise the issue loudly so everyone stops and rethinks

questions.text: You've been studying for 3 hours straight. Your best friend wants you to come play cricket. You:

options.label: Go — I need the break and social time
Say no — I finish what I start
Go for 30 minutes, then come back
Feel guilty either way

questions.text: Your teacher marks your answer wrong even though you're 100% sure you're right. You:

options.label: Politely challenge them with your reasoning
Accept it and move on to avoid conflict
Stew about it but say nothing
Double-check yourself first, then decide

questions.text: There's a new student who knows nobody. What do you do?

options.label: Walk up and introduce myself immediately
Wait to see if they seem open to talking
Get someone more social to introduce them
Smile, nod — and let them settle in on their own

questions.text: You have one week of holiday. What's your dream plan?

options.label: Road trip with a big group of friends
Read books, watch docs, learn something new
Stay home, sleep, recharge — alone
Volunteer for a cause I care about

questions.text: You're in the middle of a tough exam and your mind blanks. You:

options.label: Panic — I can feel my heart racing
Skip it, do other questions, come back calm
Take three deep breaths and try again
Accept the loss and move on

questions.text: You get a chance to redesign the school canteen menu. You:

options.label: Survey every student and make data-based decisions
Come up with wild, creative new dishes
Ask the canteen staff what's feasible
Not my thing — let someone else handle it

questions.text: A friend asks you to help them with something dishonest (copying homework). You:

options.label: Refuse and explain why it's a bad idea
Help but feel really uncomfortable
Help without thinking twice
Offer to teach them the concept instead

blocks.title: What kind of work excites you?

questions.text: If you had to pick one thing to spend a Saturday afternoon on, what would it be?

options.label: Build or fix something with your hands
Research a mystery or unsolved question
Create art, music, or write a story
Organise a get-together or help a friend
Start a small business or sell something
Make spreadsheets, plans, or organise data

questions.text: You're watching YouTube. Which video do you click first?

options.label: How a jet engine works (engineering explained)
Why humans dream — science of the brain
This artist built a house from scratch
How this 19-year-old made ₹10 lakh online
A spoken word poem that made millions cry
Documentary: the people who clean up after disasters

questions.text: School gives you a free period to work on anything. You pick:

options.label: Design a new app or gadget prototype
Write a short film script or comic strip
Organise a fundraiser for a cause
Analyse data about something you're curious about
Practice a sport or build something physical
Create a detailed plan for your future

questions.text: Which school subject do you actually enjoy most?

options.label: Physics / Chemistry / Biology
Maths
English / Literature / Languages
History / Geography / Social Studies
Business Studies / Economics
Art / Music / Drama

questions.text: You're on a school trip to a new city. You're most excited to:

options.label: Visit factories or see how things are made
Go to a museum or science centre
Visit a market and talk to vendors about their business
Explore local art, music, or theatre
Do community service or meet local NGOs
Check out coworking spaces or offices

questions.text: Your dream project is something that:

options.label: Solves a real physical problem — clean water, safer buildings
Discovers something nobody knew before
Makes people feel something deeply — a film, a song
Directly helps people in need
Builds a brand that changes an industry
Creates a perfectly organized system

questions.text: How much can your family invest in college per year (roughly)?

options.label: Under ₹1 lakh
₹1–5 lakh
₹5–15 lakh
₹15 lakh+

questions.text: What grade are you currently in?

options.label: Grade 8 or 9 (early explorer)
Grade 10 (board year coming)
Grade 11 or 12 (serious planning time)

blocks.title: What do you want your life to look like?

questions.text: Picture yourself at 30. Where are you living?

options.label: A big metro city — Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore
A growing mid-size city
Back in my hometown — I want to grow it
Wherever the work takes me

questions.text: What matters more to you in a career?

options.label: Stability — steady income, safe job
High income — I want to be wealthy
Impact — I want to change something real
Flexibility — work on my own terms

questions.text: How do you feel about risk?

options.label: I avoid it — I'd rather have something certain
Calculated risks are fine — if I've thought it through
I thrive on it — risk is exciting

questions.text: How do you feel about AI changing jobs in the future?

options.label: Worried — I want a career AI can't replace
Excited — I want to work with AI and tech
Indifferent — I'll adapt to whatever comes
